title: Why AI Coding Tools Are Making You Slower (And What Actually Works)
author: Rituraj
text:
Member-only story
Why AI Coding Tools Are Making You Slower (And What Actually Works)
I spent months testing Cursor, Antigravity, Claude Code, and Copilot. Here’s the uncomfortable truth nobody in the AI hype cycle is talking about.
I watched 200 lines of code materialize in my editor in under eight seconds. Cursor’s agent had taken my one-line prompt and generated an entire authentication module — controllers, services, middleware, the works.
I felt fast.
Forty minutes later, I was still debugging line 147.
The AI had hallucinated a middleware function that didn’t exist in my framework version. Fixing it broke the service layer. Fixing that introduced a subtle race condition I didn’t catch until my tests started failing intermittently.
I could have written the 30 lines I actually needed in 10 minutes.
That was my wake-up call. And after spending most of the past year testing every major AI coding tool on the market — Cursor, Google Antigravity, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf, Cline — while building production applications, I’ve come to a conclusion that won’t make me popular in developer Twitter circles:
